probability.last_reasoning: Shifting 2% from base to bear and 2% from base to mid. Bear increase reflects: XRP at $1.38 (well below $2 base floor), extreme fear at 14, compound stress pre-loaded, multiple data feeds degraded. Mid increase reflects: RLUSD recovery to $1.584B, institutional infrastructure buildout continuing (PermissionedDEX live, Ripple Prime, Metallicus FedNow), 7 ETFs operational. The thesis infrastructure is advancing while market conditions deteriorate — this divergence justifies widening the distribution rather than collapsing toward bear.

bear_case.score_reasoning: Elevated from baseline due to: XRP trading 31% below base case floor, extreme fear environment, 3 of 5 automated kill switches lacking data, zero PermissionedDEX adoption after 11 days live, and compound macro stress pre-loaded. Thesis infrastructure is advancing but the market is not validating it, and key metrics remain unverifiable.

competing_infrastructure.name: SWIFT GPI
competing_infrastructure.status_text: SWIFT continues incremental upgrades to GPI tracking and pre-validation. 11,500-bank network effects remain the strongest competitive moat. No XRPL integration announced.

competing_infrastructure.name: Visa B2B Connect
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Visa B2B Connect operational across 100+ countries. Enterprise adoption steady. Competes directly for the same cross-border settlement corridors XRP targets.

competing_infrastructure.name: JPMorgan Kinexys
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Kinexys (formerly Onyx) processing $2B+/day in institutional transfers. JPMorgan's balance sheet and client base give it distribution advantages Ripple cannot match. Ripple Prime paper explicitly positions against JPMorgan — acknowledges the threat.

competing_infrastructure.name: BIS Project Nexus
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Multi-central-bank initiative to interlink domestic fast payment systems. If successful, could eliminate the need for bridge currencies entirely. Kill Switch #9 tracks this. No live deployment yet but political backing is strong.

competing_infrastructure.name: Ethereum Institutional
competing_infrastructure.status_text: Ethereum L2 ecosystem expanding institutional DeFi. BlackRock BUIDL fund on Ethereum. However, settlement speed and cost still favor XRPL for high-frequency cross-border use cases.

bear_case.odl_stagnation: ODL volume data has been unavailable for an extended period — Ripple's decision to stop publishing this data is itself a bearish signal. Without volume proof, the core utility thesis remains an assertion, not a measurement.
bear_case.token_velocity_concern: Token velocity data (Kill Switch #8) remains UNKNOWN. If XRP functions purely as a bridge asset with sub-second hold times, value capture for token holders may be minimal regardless of volume growth. This is the most underexamined bear thesis dimension.

macro_headwinds.name: Global Recession Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Fear & Greed at 14, compound macro stress pre-loaded, S&P data anomalous but likely declining. Trade war via Section 122 tariffs actively depressing global growth expectations.

macro_headwinds.name: Crypto Winter Signals
macro_headwinds.status_text: XRP at $1.38 down significantly from recent highs. Extreme fear readings. However, institutional infrastructure buildout (ETFs, PermissionedDEX, Ripple Prime) is counter-cyclical — not typical of crypto winter capitulation.

macro_headwinds.name: Regulatory Reversal Risk
macro_headwinds.status_text: Clarity Act pending but SEC/CFTC regulatory posture remains favorable. 7 ETFs approved and operational. No reversal signals detected.

macro_headwinds.name: Rate Hike Extension
macro_headwinds.status_text: US 10Y at 3.97% suggests markets pricing some easing. However, BOJ tightening risk is the more relevant rate threat — a BOJ hike compresses the carry trade, triggering global liquidity withdrawal that would pressure all risk assets including XRP.

bear_case.bear_narrative: Bear pressure at 52 — thesis underwater but not drowning. Three kill switches at WARNING: ODL volume data classified as SUSPICIOUS_ABSENCE with behavioral evidence suggesting Ripple is pivoting from XRP bridge asset to diversified infrastructure provider; RLUSD stalling at $1.58B needing $11.28M/day to hit target with no visible catalyst; and the ODL transparency kill switch may not be met on its original terms because the product has evolved beyond XRP-specific metrics. The infrastructure story is real — live ETFs, multi-regulator registrations, SBI's ¥10B bonds, record on-chain activity — but the volume story that would convert infrastructure into thesis confirmation remains a black box. Compound stress is pre-loaded with all three legs deteriorating toward elevated simultaneously; one external shock (Hormuz, BOJ surprise) could cascade the macro environment from orderly to disorderly. The paradox holds: the conditions that would most stress this position are the same conditions that most validate the need for alternative settlement infrastructure. INCREASE_MONITORING with Q2 2026 as the next decision gate — if RLUSD doesn't accelerate and ODL data doesn't surface, the recommendation hardens.
